iterations.hypothesis: Use BTC/USDC (listing 1) microprice as the primary fair value reference for quoting BTC/USDE (listing 28). BTC/USDC has higher volume and should give a more reliable price signal. BTC/USDE spread volatility (EWMA-smoothed) sizes the half-spread. min_spread_bps=3.0 to cover the 1.2 bps maker fee on each side.
iterations.analysis: USDC FV caused structural bias: $20 USDE-USDC premium put our asks below the USDE market mid, generating 128 adversely-selected ask fills at -1.687 bps edge.
iterations.next_action: Switch to USDE own MicropriceFairValue to eliminate basis-driven adverse selection.

iterations.hypothesis: Switch FV to BTC/USDE own microprice. Removes basis bias.
iterations.analysis: Both sides adversely selected ~0.55 bps average. USDE spread is 0.125 bps; fills happen during price sweeps. Each fill costs ~1.75 bps (1.2 maker fee + 0.55 adverse selection).
iterations.next_action: Try flow imbalance skew to detect toxic flow.

iterations.hypothesis: Add flow_bps adjustment to reduce adverse selection.
iterations.analysis: Disaster: flow_bps=2.0 > min_half=1.25 bps shifted reservation past mid, turning passive orders into aggressive market orders. 1985 taker fills at 4 bps each.
iterations.next_action: Remove flow signal. Try spread gate filter.

iterations.hypothesis: Wide USDE spreads indicate dislocation opportunity; only quote when spread > 3 bps.
iterations.analysis: Backfired: wide spreads occur during book sweeps = maximum adverse selection. Bid edge worsened from -0.504 to -1.861 bps.
iterations.next_action: Switch to USDC+basis_ewma FV for stat-arb mean-reversion edge.

iterations.hypothesis: FV = USDC + EWMA(basis). Fill when USDE deviates, profit from basis reversion. gamma=1,000,000 for A-S skew.
iterations.analysis: Catastrophic: gamma*pos*vol_raw^2/fv explodes when vol_bps=10 during sweeps. Skew=40,000,000 → bid at $11.4M. Root cause: SpreadVolatility squared makes A-S skew explosively sensitive.
iterations.next_action: Replace A-S with linear skew. Keep USDC+basis FV.

iterations.hypothesis: Replace A-S gamma with linear skew (skew_per_btc_bps * pos * fv / 10000). FV = USDC + basis_ewma (alpha=0.9990). This generates profitable fills when USDE deviates from USDC+basis.
iterations.analysis: First positive PnL (+$20)! But only 29 intents and 5 fills because FV = int(usdc_fv + basis_ewma) rarely changes integer value (basis_ewma changes by 0.001/tick). Sticky quotes problem.
iterations.next_action: Blend USDE (70%) with USDC+basis (30%) so USDE dominates and changes every tick, while USDC+basis provides reversion edge.

iterations.hypothesis: Blend fv = 0.7*usde_fv + 0.3*(usdc_fv + basis_ewma). USDE dominates for quote dynamism; USDC+basis provides mean-reversion edge. basis_alpha=0.99 for ~6-min half-life.
iterations.analysis: OMS unit bug: after 0.03115 BTC ask fill, oms.get_effective_quantity() returned -31,150 (micro-BTC). skew = 2.0*(-31150)*80447/10000 = -501,183 → reservation = 581,630 → bid at $581K → filled at market → -$50K.
iterations.next_action: Divide OMS position by 1e6 to convert to BTC before skew and guard calculation.

iterations.hypothesis: Clamp position used for skew to [-max_position, max_position] to prevent OMS unit-scale bugs from exploding skew.
changes: Added skew_pos = max(-max_pos, min(max_pos, position)) clamping
iterations.analysis: 100% taker fills. Clamping position_raw to [-5, 5] always produces maximum skew (±80 >> half_spread=12), causing all quotes to cross the spread.
iterations.next_action: Divide OMS position by 1e6 to get BTC, set max_position=0.5 BTC, use unfavorable price guard.

iterations.hypothesis: Fix OMS units: position_btc = oms_position / 1e6. Use max_position=0.5 BTC with unfavorable-price guard (bid_price=fv*0.5 when long, ask_price=fv*2 when short) to avoid framework slot-collision crash from size=0 orders.
changes: Guard uses unfavorable price instead of size=0
iterations.analysis: All thresholds met but targets far from met. 97.6% apparent taker fills (measurement artifact: fill_price vs book_state AFTER market sweep). $55 PnL from BTC appreciation on accumulated 0.67 BTC long, not edge. Only day 1 fills.
iterations.next_action: Remove USDC blend from FV entirely; use fv = usde_fv only. Use basis deviation as reservation skew adjustment.

iterations.hypothesis: Remove USDC blend from FV entirely. fv = USDE microprice only (no lag). Use basis deviation as reservation skew: basis_skew = basis_skew_weight * (current_basis - basis_ewma). When USDE expensive vs history, reservation falls (aggressive selling); when cheap, reservation rises (aggressive buying).
changes: FV = USDE microprice only (no USDC blend)
Added basis_skew = 0.1 * basis_dev as reservation adjustment
iterations.analysis: Bid edge improved dramatically. Still accumulates 0.67 BTC long by 10:50 on day 1, guard triggers, no fills day 2. $106 PnL = BTC appreciation on position, not trading edge.
iterations.next_action: Increase max_position to 1.0 BTC so strategy continues quoting through both days.

iterations.hypothesis: Increase max_position to 1.0 BTC so the guard triggers later, allowing fills throughout both days.
iterations.analysis: Bid edge dramatically improved to -0.035 bps (passive fills at fair value). 148 fills but only day 1, guard triggers at max_position=1.0. $646 = ~$152 realized + ~$494 BTC appreciation on 1.03 BTC long. Sharpe still 300x below target.
iterations.next_action: Widen spread to 5.0 bps and increase basis_skew_weight to 0.5 for stronger mean-reversion signal.

iterations.hypothesis: Widen spread to 5 bps (1.3 bps net per fill) and increase basis_skew_weight to 0.5 for stronger mean-reversion signal.
iterations.analysis: PnL jumped to $1,580 (+2.4x), sharpe 0.0077 (+2.6x vs iter_011). Position flipped to -1.01 BTC SHORT. 129 fills only on day 1; guard triggers at max_position. PnL dominated by BTC directional position (~$1,058), not spread capture (~$522). Sharpe 130x below target.
iterations.next_action: Tighten max_position to 0.2 BTC and widen spread to 7 bps to reduce BTC directional exposure.

iterations.hypothesis: Dramatically tighten max_position to 0.2 BTC and widen spread to 7 bps. Limits BTC beta exposure to ±0.2 BTC; captures 2.3 bps net per fill. Position should oscillate in [-0.2, +0.2], reducing variance from BTC directional beta.
iterations.analysis: Catastrophically fewer fills (31 vs 129). All fills on May 13 01:11-11:03; strategy went dormant for 37 hours after accumulating +0.25 BTC long (above max_position=0.2). Mean edge -0.807 bps (adverse selection). pct_positive_buckets=0.4 fails threshold. Tight max_position creates stuck-long problem: guard prevents more bids, but asks don't get hit in uptrending market. Confirms that tight max_position is not the solution — it just kills activity without improving sharpe. BTC market spread is ~1 bps; at any skew level consistent with passive quotes, position accumulation is driven by BTC trend direction, not solvable purely through skew.
iterations.next_action: Parameter sweep (batch): strategy logic is stable since iter_010. Sweep max_position=[0.3,0.5,1.0], skew_per_btc_bps=[3,5,8], min_spread_bps=[5,8,12,16] to find combo that maximizes fills throughout both days while reducing position variance. Higher skew + wider spread = faster mean-reversion without taker fills.
